text: Increased uterine weight is physiological development associated with reproductive maturity of an individual, with organisms exposed to a stressor exhibiting increase in uterine weight at an abnormal age or to an unusual level (Goldman et al. 2008).  Uterine weight increases due to proliferation of cells, particularly endometrial epithelial (lining) cells, causing an increase in uterine lining thickness (Nicklaus et al. 2007).
